commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: The remaining blocker is contributor real-behavior proof and normal maintainer review, not an automated code repair.; Cleared: The focused current diff touches only diffs viewer source, tests, and the generated runtime; I found no concrete security or supply-chain regression. (sha=7f0571d04f5a28b1ae4333a3a3d80ab42507f5f6)
commands.review_summary:
The PR isolates per-card diffs viewer hydration failures, updates the generated viewer runtime, and adds jsdom regression coverage for continuing after one card fails.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main has an unguarded hydrateViewer card loop, so a synchronous FileDiff hydrate/setOptions failure in one card exits before later cards; I verified this by source inspection rather than executing the repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is contributor real-behavior proof and normal maintainer review, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(codex): recover final text after prompt timeout
target.body:
Summary
- Recover a completed final assistant text payload when a prompt timeout races Codex app-server completion and the runner already has final `stop`/`end_turn`/`completed` assistant text.
- Keep pure prompt silence, or timeout cases without successful final assistant text, on the existing timeout error path.
- Add regression coverage and a changelog entry for the #84516 Form B failure mode.

Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 timeout 300s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts -t "prompt timeout races completion|strict-agentic blocked exit|empty assistant"`
- `CI=1 timeout 300s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts`
- AWS Crabbox fresh PR focused regression on `c4f3de33346b0c5bf8120ec473f0ee08d9d811b9`: `run_04d5b2255fed` / `cbx_c13825905dfc` passed.
- AWS Crabbox fresh PR changed gate on `c4f3de33346b0c5bf8120ec473f0ee08d9d811b9`: `run_75e05edd563a` / `cbx_db171cdf8b82` passed `pnpm check:changed -- CHANGELOG.md src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts`.
- Blacksmith Testbox: first run `tbx_01ks5hcrs4fr53ebnn8thsybqd` found a lint issue before the amend; after the amend, `tbx_01ks5hmhf173mzsmrjgz3f5h0p`, `tbx_01ks5hwd53t9dj88d6q2075sr3`, and `tbx_01ks5j3h0rrdxn3yd2f3b3y9n4` failed in the Blacksmith rsync/sync layer before running the gate, so AWS Crabbox fresh-PR `check:changed` is the broad proof for this head.

Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Codex app-server timeout race where final assistant text is captured with a successful `stop`/`end_turn`/`completed` finish, but payload projection is empty and downstream channels receive an empty timeout envelope.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw focused runner tests in a fresh gwt worktree; AWS Crabbox fresh PR Linux runs against this PR head.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the focused timeout-race regression, the full incomplete-turn runner suite, AWS Crabbox fresh-PR focused regression, and AWS Crabbox fresh-PR `pnpm check:changed` on the touched files.
Evidence after fix: the new regression returns `payloads: [{ text: finalText }]`, preserves `finalAssistantVisibleText`/`finalAssistantRawText`, reports liveness `working`, and records the execution attempt as assistant-stage success.
Observed result after fix: completed final assistant text is promoted into the terminal payload path instead of being replaced by a timeout error when completion and prompt timeout race.
What was not tested: live reporter gpt-5.5 OAuth long reply / Telegram outbound; Blacksmith Testbox changed gate after the amend because the Testbox rsync/sync layer failed before executing the command. The Form A pure-silence case with no assistant text remains timeout behavior by design.

Refs #84516.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.author: cosmopolitan033

target.title: fix(diffs): continue after card hydration errors
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep hydrating remaining diff cards when one card fails during `FileDiff` construction or `hydrate()`.
- Log a per-card warning instead of letting one failure reject the whole viewer hydration path.
- Add jsdom coverage proving a later card still hydrates after the first card throws.

Fixes #83914

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `hydrateViewer` previously ran each `.oc-diff-card` hydration in one unguarded loop. If one card threw during `FileDiff` construction, `hydrate()`, or state application, the exception propagated out of `hydrateViewer` and later cards were not hydrated.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, Node.js v22.22.0, branch `publish/diffs-continue-after-card-hydration-error-83914`, using jsdom plus the real `extensions/diffs/src/viewer-client.ts` hydration loop under the repo's Vitest wrapper.

Exact steps or command run after fix:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/diffs/src/viewer-client.test.ts
$ git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix: Targeted test output:

```console
✓ extension-diffs ../../extensions/diffs/src/viewer-client.test.ts (1 test) 77ms

Test Files 1 passed (1)
Tests 1 passed (1)
```

Observed result after fix: The regression test renders two diff cards, makes the first `hydrate()` call throw, then verifies `hydrate()` is still invoked for the second card, one controller is registered for the successful card, and the page-level `openclawDiffsError` flag is not set by the per-card failure.

What was not tested: I did not run a full browser artifact build or open the generated viewer HTML in a real browser; the targeted jsdom test exercises the failing per-card hydration seam directly.

## Test plan
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/diffs/src/viewer-client.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
target.author: cosmopolitan033
